title: 10 best herbal creams for gastroparesis

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which may contribute to gastroparesis symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

Digestive stimulant properties: The natural oils and extracts present in herbal ginger creams, such as ginger oil and turmeric, can stimulate digestion and improve gastric motility, helping to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis like bloating, nausea, and slow stomach emptying.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds can help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to gastroparesis symptoms like abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with gastroparesis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to stimulate digestive enzymes. The cream's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to improve gut motility and reduce nausea, which are common symptoms of gastroparesis. By applying the cream topically, it can help to relax the stomach muscles and increase blood flow to the digestive tract, promoting a smoother and more efficient digestion process. This can lead to improved nutrient absorption and reduced symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_constituents: Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating gastroparesis symptoms.
Gingerols: Gingerols have gastric motility-enhancing properties, which can stimulate the contraction of the stomach muscles, helping to improve gastric emptying and alleviate gastroparesis symptoms.

6-Gingerol: 6-Gingerol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating gastroparesis symptoms and promoting overall gut health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used for their ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea, making them a suitable choice for treating gastroparesis.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain similar compounds to the rhyzome and roots, providing anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits that can help manage gastroparesis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh zingiber officinale roots with a sharp knife in the morning.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 35-40°c for 24-48 hours in a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2-3 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of coconut oil with 50 grams of the ground root powder in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Irritant properties: Peppermint's irritant properties help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of gastroparesis, such as bloating and discomfort, by increasing gut motility.
Antispasmodic effects: The antispasmodic compounds in peppermint cream help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with gastroparesis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with gastroparesis because of its ability to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, improving the movement of food through the stomach. The menthol in peppermint creams stimulates the digestive system, enhancing the release of digestive enzymes and improving nutrient absorption. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the stomach, further alleviating gastroparesis symptoms. This natural remedy provides relief for individuals experiencing delayed stomach emptying and related discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has been shown to help with gastroparesis by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and increasing gastric emptying, which can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Limonene: Limonene has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as abdominal pain and discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, potentially alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for gastroparesis due to their high content of menthol, which helps to alleviate nausea and digestive discomfort.
Roots: They are used to make creams for gastroparesis due to their high content of menthone and other compounds that help to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms.
Stems: They are used to make creams for gastroparesis due to their ability to help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, thereby reducing symptoms of nausea and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the early morning to ensure maximum essential oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place for 2-3 days or until they reach a constant weight.
Steep 20g of the dried leaves in 200ml of carrier oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal infused oil.
Mix 100g of the herbal infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis.

COX-2 inhibition: Curcumin's ability to inhibit COX-2 enzyme production reduces prostaglandin synthesis, leading to decreased gastric acid secretion and inflammation, which can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with gastroparesis.

Gastroprotective activity: Turmeric has been shown to have gastroprotective properties, including reducing gastric ulcers and mucosal damage, which can help prevent complications such as bleeding and perforation in individuals with gastroparesis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with gastroparesis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, has been shown to improve digestive motility and reduce inflammation in the stomach and small intestine. By alleviating symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, turmeric creams provide relief for individuals with gastroparesis. Additionally, curcumin has been found to increase digestive enzyme production, helping to break down food more efficiently and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps with gastroparesis by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and improving digestion, which can alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect the stomach lining from damage and promoting the healing of gastric ulcers, which can contribute to gastroparesis.

Tumerone (α-Turmerone): A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the stomach and promote the motility of the gastrointestinal tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and digestive-aiding properties.
Roots: Roots are also widely used for their rich content of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
Barks: Barks are sometimes used in combination with rhyzomes and roots to enhance the overall efficacy of the cream, although their contribution is less significant.
remedy_recipe: Infuse curcuma longa in a carrier oil by combining 200 grams of oil with 50 grams of dried root for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the oil from the solids.
Combine the infused oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce oxidative stress, both of which can contribute to gastroparesis.

Saponins: These compounds can help to increase the production of stomach acid and improve digestive enzymes, which can aid in proper digestion and absorption of nutrients and help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with gastroparesis because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive tract. remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea and vomiting.

Licoricidin: A phenolic compound, licoricidin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help protect the stomach lining from damage and promote the healing of gastric ulcers, a common complication of gastroparesis.

Licopyrone: A flavonoid glycoside, licopyrone has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as delayed gastric emptying and abdominal pain.

Barks: Employed for their purported ability to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis due to their bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a 5% yield of glycyrrhizin.
Combine the harvested roots with 2 liters of water in a large pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 2 hours then strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove solids.
Mix 100 grams of cetearyl alcohol 10 grams of cetyl alcohol and 20 grams of glycerin with 200 ml of distilled water.
Combine the herbal infusion from step 3 with the mixture from step 4 and stir until a smooth cream forms.

remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel cream's carminative property helps to relieve bloating and gas associated with gastroparesis by reducing the formation of flatulence and improving digestion.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of fennel cream help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can occur due to gastroparesis, leading to improved gut motility and reduced discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with gastroparesis because of its unique properties that aid digestion and alleviate symptoms of the condition. The anethole compound found in fennel has a natural antispasmodic effect, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and prevent cramps and nausea. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to reduce gas and bloating, allowing food to pass through the digestive system more efficiently, providing relief to individuals suffering from gastroparesis. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a phenolic compound found in Foeniculum vulgare, may help alleviate gastroparesis symptoms by enhancing gastric motility and relaxing the smooth muscle in the gastrointestinal tract.
Anethol: Anethol, a terpene derived from Foeniculum vulgare, may help reduce gastroparesis symptoms by soothing the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and spasms, and promoting relaxation.

Beta-pinene: Beta-pinene, another terpene present in Foeniculum vulgare, may help alleviate gastroparesis symptoms by reducing inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, promoting relaxation, and enhancing gastric motility.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Their essential oil, particularly anethole, is used to alleviate digestive issues and relieve symptoms of gastroparesis.
Leaves: Their essential oil has been used to treat various gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and indigestion associated with gastroparesis.
Roots: They are a common source of inulin, a prebiotic that can help regulate gut bacteria and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from mature plants at dawn or late afternoon.
Steam distill the harvested material at 100°c for 2 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine 10 grams of the essential oil with 90 grams of jojoba oil and 20 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth paste.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2-4 hours before transferring it to a storage container.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms of gastroparesis.

Bitters and Digestive Stimulants: The bitter compounds present in dandelion cream stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, helping to regulate digestion and alleviate gastroparesis symptoms like bloating and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh dandelion flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried for best results.
Steam distill the dandelion flowers at 100c for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Mix 1 tablespoon of dandelion essential oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 10 grams of aloe vera gel and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and blend until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Slippery elm cream creates a soothing, protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, helping to calm inflammation and irritation caused by gastroparesis, allowing the stomach to function more efficiently.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in slippery elm cream help reduce swelling and pain associated with gastroparesis, allowing for improved digestion and reducing symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Glycosaminoglycan content: Slippery elm contains glycosaminoglycans (GAGs), which are complex carbohydrates that help restore the natural mucous barrier in the digestive tract, promoting healthy gut function and reducing inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with gastroparesis because of its soothing and protective properties. The mucilages present in Ulmus rubra have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the healing of mucous membranes. This can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has antioxidant properties that may help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and promote healing, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of gastroparesis.

remedy_parts: Barks: Ulmus rubra barks are used because they contain tannins and other compounds that may help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
Leaves: Ulmus rubra leaves are used due to their content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which may help regulate gut motility and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
Stems: Ulmus rubra stems are used because they contain compounds that may help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, thereby improving gut motility and reducing symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried ulmus rubra bark and 200g of dried leaves from the plant.
Grind the dried bark and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 250g of jojoba oil, 50g of beeswax, and 20g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Add the ground ulmus rubra powder to the melted jojoba oil mixture and stir for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow cream contains high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective gel-like substance that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastroparesis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and small intestine, alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

Prebiotic properties: Marshmallow cream's mucilage can also act as a prebiotic, helping to feed beneficial bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and improving digestion, which can be compromised in individuals with gastroparesis.

remedy_constituents: Mucilage: A complex mixture of polysaccharides that forms a protective barrier in the digestive system, helping to soothe and calm inflammation in the stomach and intestines, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis.

Polysaccharides (mainly galactomannans and rhamnogalacturonans): These components help to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, facilitating the movement of food through the digestive system and reducing symptoms of gastroparesis.

Iridoid glycosides (mainly althaeoside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and protect the digestive tract.
Barks: The barks of Althaea officinalis contain mucilage, which, when applied topically, can help relieve digestive discomfort and promote healing.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis are used for their mucilage content, which can aid in digestion and provide relief from gastroparesis symptoms when applied topically.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried roots of althaea officinalis from a trusted supplier.
Combine the dried roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix 20-30 grams of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms.

Gastroprotective properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba extracts may have gastroprotective effects by reducing gastric acid secretion, inflammation, and damage caused by NSAIDs or other irritants, which could potentially help manage gastroparesis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with gastroparesis because of its unique ability to enhance blood flow and reduce inflammation. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba creams have been shown to improve digestion by increasing gut motility and reducing gastric stasis. This, in turn, helps to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba help with gastroparesis by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby enhancing digestion and reducing symptoms associated with gastroparesis.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a triterpene in Ginkgo biloba, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with gastroparesis, and improve overall digestive function.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a phenolic compound in Ginkgo biloba, has antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress in the digestive tract, alleviate inflammation and improve digestive function, thereby providing relief from symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their bioactive compounds and flavonoids, which are believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the digestive system.
Seeds: They are used because they contain ginkgolides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and smooth muscle relaxant properties, potentially beneficial for gastroparesis symptoms.
Buds: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 10 grams of beeswax in a stainless steel bowl.
Melt 10 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at 150-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Steep 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 50 grams of distilled water for 5 hours at 95-105 degrees fahrenheit then strain.
Combine the melted oils with the strained ginkgo biloba infusion and add 30 grams of aloe vera gel in a mixing bowl.
Mix the ginkgo biloba cream with 10 grams of vitamin e oil and pour the mixture into 100-gram glass jars for 5 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of ripe capsicum annuum fruits from well-maintained gardens with optimal sunlight.
Dry the harvested capsicum annuum fruits in a food dehydrator at 135 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Grind 1/2 cup of dried capsicum annuum into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder.
Mix 1/4 cup of the powdered capsicum annuum with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the resulting herbal capsicum annuum cream in a glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with gastroparesis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate digestive discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar and stir well to create an infusion mixture.
Allow the infusion mixture to steep for 48 hours at room temperature, shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate blending and extraction.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.

Mix one part of the matricaria chamomilla infusion with three parts of beeswax and two drops of vitamin e essential oil, melting them together in a double boiler at 100°f for 30 minutes to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place.
Grind 20g of dried valeriana officinalis root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 30g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low heat for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before storage for up to six months.

Buds: The buds contain a unique combination of flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to stimulate digestion, relieve cramps, and reduce nausea associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh populus balsamifera bark, drying it for 48 hours before grinding into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered bark with 20% glycerin and 80% petroleum jelly in a double boiler, heating until the mixture reaches 120°f over 30 minutes.
Stir the cooled mixture well, adding 5 drops of peppermint essential oil for its digestive benefits and anti-inflammatory properties.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers, labeling and storing them in the refrigerator at 40°f to ensure optimal potency for 6 months.
Apply a thin layer of the populus balsamifera cream topically to the abdominal area twice daily, massaging gently for 2 minutes each time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain a high concentration of allantoin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that soothes the digestive tract. Allantoin reduces inflammation and irritation in the gut, allowing the muscles to contract and relax properly, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, bloating, and abdominal pain. Additionally, comfrey's demulcent properties form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, shielding them from further irritation and promoting healing. This natural cream provides relief and comfort for individuals suffering from gastroparesis.
remedy_properties: Allyl sulfides: Comfrey cream contains allyl sulfides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve gut motility, helping to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
Allantoin: Allantoin is a natural anti-inflammatory compound found in comfrey that can help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with gastroparesis.

Saponins: Saponins are compounds found in comfrey that have been shown to improve gut function by enhancing digestive enzyme activity, reducing inflammation, and promoting mucosal healing, all of which can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues associated with gastroparesis.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of comfrey contain allantoin, a natural compound that promotes tissue healing and reduces inflammation in the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality symphytum officinale leaves in a sunny area after dew has dried for maximum potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or air-drying them for 3 days.
Combine 20g of dried symphytum officinale leaves with 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids, and allow it to cool at room temperature for 2 hours.
Whip 200g of beeswax with 100ml of coconut oil in a mixing bowl until smooth, then slowly pour in the infused oil mixture while whisking continuously for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to alleviate symptoms. The antispasmodic properties of these herbs, such as marshmallow and slippery elm, help to relax the muscles of the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, allowing food to move more smoothly through the stomach and intestines. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the creams may help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the gastrointestinal lining, further improving digestion and reducing gastroparesis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycans: Herbal grape creams often contain glycans, which can help to break down and absorb nutrients more efficiently, alleviating digestive issues associated with gastroparesis.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic compounds present in herbal grape creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as bloating, cramps, and abdominal pain.

Fiber: Many herbal grape creams contain fiber-rich ingredients like psyllium or flaxseed, which can help regulate bowel movements, manage constipation, and improve overall digestive health, making it easier for the body to process nutrients in individuals with gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Grape roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues associated with gastroparesis.
Fruits: The fruits of the grape plant contain antioxidants and anthocyanins, which help to reduce inflammation and improve digestion in patients with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality vitis vinifera leaves and flowers in the early morning and dry them thoroughly within 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried vitis vinifera leaves with 10g of dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herbal blend with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat it gently for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep for 2 hours before straining it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar.
Add 10% of vitamin e oil as an emulsifier to the herbal infusion and mix well before filling sterilized cream jars with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain compounds that stimulate gastric motility, promoting healthy digestion and gut function. The herbal extracts in maypop creams, such as passionflower and lemon balm, have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and calm the stomach. By enhancing gastric motility, maypop creams can help move food through the digestive system more efficiently, alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Maypop creams containing Passiflora incarnata can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with gastroparesis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and alkaloids present in maypop extracts have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining that can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms.

Anxiolytic properties: Maypop creams containing Passiflora incarnata have been traditionally used to treat anxiety and stress, both of which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and improving digestion associated with gastroparesis.
Barks: The barks of the Maypop plant contain tannins and other compounds that help to soothe and protect the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms such as bloating and abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passionflower herb passiflora incarnata in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 10g of dried passionflower herb with 20g of coconut oil and heat mixture at 60°c for 4 hours.
Strain the heated mixture through cheesecloth to separate the infused oil from the solids then discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with 30g of beeswax and heat until melted and well combined.
Pour the melted mixture into small jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with gastroparesis because they can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of delayed gastric emptying. The fatty acid content in saw palmetto has been shown to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can exacerbate muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines. By blocking this conversion, saw palmetto creams may help reduce muscle spasms and improve digestion in individuals with gastroparesis. This natural approach can provide relief from symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Saw palmetto has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
Digestive soothe: The herb has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help ease cramping, bloating, and other uncomfortable symptoms associated with gastroparesis.

Muscle relaxant: Saw palmetto's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue in the digestive tract may help improve gastric motility (the movement of food through the stomach) and reduce the severity of gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saw palmetto roots contain flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate gastrointestinal symptoms associated with gastroparesis.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including gastroparesis, due to their ability to improve gut motility and relieve symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried serenoa repens leaves measure out 20 grams for this recipe.
Combine dried serenoa repens leaves with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Add 30 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the mixture stir well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes or until it reaches 65 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat let cool to room temperature then whip until creamy texture forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain isoflavones, specifically formononetin and biochanin A, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. This relief from muscle spasms and inflammation can help ease symptoms of gastroparesis, such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Additionally, the creams may also improve gut motility by reducing smooth muscle contractions and increasing peristalsis, allowing for more efficient digestion and elimination of waste.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover cream contains isoflavones, which have been shown to improve gut motility and reduce symptoms of gastroparesis by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines.

Anthocyanins: The anthocyanins present in red clover cream have antioxidant properties that help protect the gut lining from inflammation and damage, reducing the risk of gastroparesis and related symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Fiber content: Red clover cream contains a type of fiber called pectin, which can help regulate bowel movements and improve digestion in individuals with gastroparesis by providing bulk to stool and preventing constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, vitamins, and antioxidants, which can help to lubricate the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried trifolium pratense flowers and chop them into small pieces.
Combine chopped flowers with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax, vitamin e oil, and other ingredients such as aloe vera gel.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with gastroparesis because they contain anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that soothe the stomach lining and relax digestive muscles. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and spasms in the stomach, allowing food to move more efficiently through the digestive system. This relief from discomfort and inflammation can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, including nausea, vomiting, bloating, and abdominal pain, promoting a healthier and more balanced digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as bloating, abdominal pain, and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Yarrow cream's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining and worsen gastroparesis symptoms, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing oxidative stress.

Stomach-soothing property: Yarrow cream's stomach-soothing property helps to calm the digestive system, reducing spasms and contractions that can impede digestion and exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
Seeds: Containing natural oils that promote gut health, reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting, and provide relief from abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried achillea millefolium leaves from a trusted source.
Measure out 20 grams of dried leaves and steep them in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding solids.
Combine the resulting infusion with 50 grams of beeswax, 25 grams of coconut oil, and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler, heating until melted.
Remove from heat, let cool to around 60 degrees celsius, and whip the mixture until it thickens into a smooth cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gastroparesis are topical ointments infused with natural herbs that help alleviate symptoms of the condition. They work by improving digestion, reducing nausea, and enhancing nutrient absorption. Examples of herbal creams that help with gastroparesis include ginger, peppermint, and chamomile-based creams. These creams can improve lives by reducing pain and discomfort associated with gastroparesis, allowing individuals to enjoy daily activities and meals without discomfort, and promoting overall digestive health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastroparesis that are treated with herbal creams are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and leaky gut syndrome. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and licorice root can help soothe digestive discomforts such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. These natural remedies can also reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from symptoms similar to those experienced with gastroparesis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with gastroparesis is a blend of slippery elm, licorice root, and aloe vera. Slippery elm soothes the digestive tract, while licorice root reduces inflammation and calms the stomach muscles. Aloe vera adds a calming effect, reducing nausea and discomfort. Applying a mixture of these creams topically, specifically to the abdomen, may help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. Consult a healthcare professional before using any new creams.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, herbal creams may be beneficial for gastroparesis due to their anti-ulcerogenic properties. The study assessed the gastroprotective effects of five herbal remedies, including Phlomis grandiflora and Rosa canina, which exhibited statistically significant gastroprotective effects of 100% and 100%, respectively, in an EtOH-induced ulcerogenesis model in rats. This suggests that these herbal remedies may help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, such as ulcers and nausea, by protecting the stomach lining and promoting healing.
